Article.ArticleTitle: [Therapy for an overactive bladder in 5 minutes? The INKA app makes it possible].

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: CUROS Urologisches Zentrum, Köln, Deutschland. michaliknora@gmail.com.

Article.VernacularTitle: Therapie der überaktiven Harnblase in 5 min? Die INKA-App macht’s möglich.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Einhaltung ethischer Richtlinien. Interessenkonflikt: N. Michalik hat für die Erstellung dieser Arbeit ein Honorar von Dr. Pfleger Arzneimittel, Deutschland, erhalten. Für diesen Beitrag wurden von den Autor/-innen keine Studien an Menschen oder Tieren durchgeführt. Für die aufgeführten Studien gelten die jeweils dort angegebenen ethischen Richtlinien.

Reference.Citation: Irwin DE, Milsom I, Hunskaar S et al (2006) Population-based survey of urinary incontinence, overactive bladder, and other lower urinary tract symptoms in five countries: results of the EPIC study. Eur Urol 50(6):1306–1315. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.eururo.2006.09.019
Kinsey D, Pretorius S, Glover L, Alexander T (2016) The psychological impact of overactive bladder: a systematic review. J Health Psychol 21(1):69–81. https://doi.org/10.1177/1359105314522084

Coyne KS, Sexton CC, Kopp ZS, Ebel-Bitoun C, Milsom I, Chapple C (2011) The impact of overactive bladder on mental health, work productivity and health-related quality of life in the UK and Sweden: results from EpiLUTS. BJU Int 108(9):1459–1471. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1464-410X.2010.10013.x

Naumann G, Aigmüller T, Bader W et al (2023) Diagnosis and Therapy of Female Urinary Incontinence. Guideline of the DGGG, OEGGG and SGGG (S2k-Level, AWMF Registry No. 015/091, January 2022): Part 1 with Recommendations on Diagnostics and Conservative and Medical Treatment. Geburtshilfe Frauenheilkd 83(4):377–409. https://doi.org/10.1055/a-1967-1726
EAU Guidelines. Edn. presented at the EAU Annual Congress, Madrid 2025. ISBN 978-94-92671-29‑5.

Dumoulin C, Cacciari LP, Hay-Smith EJC (2018) Pelvic floor muscle training versus no treatment, or inactive control treatments, for urinary incontinence in women. Cochrane Database Syst Rev. https://doi.org/10.1002/14651858.CD005654.pub4

Monteiro S, Riccetto C, Araújo A, Galo L, Brito N, Botelho S (2018) Efficacy of pelvic floor muscle training in women with overactive bladder syndrome: a systematic review. Int Urogynecol J 29(11):1565–1573. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00192-018-3602-x

Bo K, Fernandes ACNL, Duarte TB, Brito LGO, Ferreira CHJ (2020) Is pelvic floor muscle training effective for symptoms of overactive bladder in women? A systematic review. Physiotherapy 106:65–76. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.physio.2019.08.011
Willis-Gray MG, Dieter AA, Geller EJ (2016) Evaluation and management of overactive bladder: strategies for optimizing care. Res Rep Urol 8:113–122. https://doi.org/10.2147/RRU.S93636

Chapple CR, Nazir J, Hakimi Z et al (2017) Persistence and adherence with mirabegron versus antimuscarinic agents in patients with overactive bladder: a retrospective observational study in UK clinical practice. Eur Urol 72(3):389–399. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.eururo.2017.01.037
Benner JS, Nichol MB, Rovner ES et al (2010) Patient-reported reasons for discontinuing overactive bladder medication. bju Int 105(9):1276–1282. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1464-410X.2009.09036.x

Watson PW, McKinstry B (2009) A systematic review of interventions to improve recall of medical advice in healthcare consultations. J R Soc Med 102(6):235–243. https://doi.org/10.1258/jrsm.2009.090013
Daten des GKV-Spitzenverbandes gem. § 33a Abs. 6 SGB V; Datenjahr 2024; n = 422.771; amtliche KM 6‑Statistik Abbildung der Inanspruchnahme auf anonymisierter Fallebene
